summary: Closed, unmerged PR that retuned src/melee/lb/lbheap.c for lbheap matching. It rewrote lbHeap_80015900 around heap create/destroy helpers, corrected the heap bounds scan to cover heaps 2-5, and changed lbHeap_80015F3C setup to preserve/achieve full matching. The author reported lbHeap_80015900 improving from 94.3% to 96.5% while keeping lbHeap_80015F3C at 100%; the decomp.dev bot reported +544 matched bytes overall, lbHeap_80015F3C becoming a 100% match, and lbHeap_80015900 improving to 96.48%.

key_files.role: Only changed file; matching and heap-bound logic updates for lbHeap_80015900 and lbHeap_80015F3C.

what_changed: Named the formerly anonymous heap descriptor struct as struct lbHeap_HeapDesc and kept lbHeap_803BA380 as a 5-entry descriptor table ending with idx 6.
Added struct lbHeap_HeapOffsetView with a 0x10-byte pad before struct Heap, plus lbHeap_GetHeapOffsetView(offset), to access heap entries through byte offsets from lbHeap_80431FA0.
Added static inline helpers lbHeap_ResetHeap, lbHeap_DestroyOffsetViewIfCreated, and lbHeap_CreateOffsetViewIfDestroyed for repeated heap initialization and create/destroy state transitions.

Reworked lbHeap_80015900 to destroy transient heaps 2-5 through offset views, use u32 arena/ARAM bounds values, set up main_heap and aram_heap pointers, then recreate non-transient heaps 2-5 through the new helper.
Fixed the heap bounds scan in lbHeap_80015900: the old TODO-described loop checked heaps 2 and 3 then 2 and 1; the new two-iteration loop processes heap_array entries 2/3 and 4/5.
Reworked lbHeap_80015F3C to call lbHeap_ResetHeap explicitly for heap_array[0] through heap_array[5], then walk lbHeap_803BA380 with a descriptor pointer desc++ instead of an index variable.
smart_moves: The offset-view struct and lbHeap_GetHeapOffsetView macro model the global layout directly, a useful matching tactic when normal heap_array indexing does not produce the desired codegen.
Factoring create/destroy/reset logic into small static inline helpers reduced repeated field code while still allowing the compiler to inline match-friendly sequences.
The bounds scan fix improved the actual modeled behavior at the same time as the match: it now covers the intended reserved/user heaps 2-5 instead of the old incorrect scan noted by the removed TODO.
Pointer-walking lbHeap_803BA380 with desc++ mirrors a common compiler pattern for sentinel tables and helped keep lbHeap_80015F3C at 100%.
Verification explicitly checked both the improved unmatched function lbHeap_80015900 and the already-sensitive lbHeap_80015F3C, reducing risk of regressing nearby matching.
decomp_lessons: For heap/global-layout code, matching may require representing an interior view of a global struct with padding and byte offsets rather than relying only on high-level array indexing.

When assembly treats addresses as integer arithmetic, using u32 temporaries for arena_lo, arena_hi, aram_lo, and aram_hi and casting only at call sites can be more match-friendly than carrying pointer-typed locals throughout.
Do not leave known-wrong loop reconstructions in place just for partial matching; correcting the control structure can both improve semantic accuracy and raise the match percentage.
Small static inline wrappers are reusable for repeated status/type/id/handle transitions when the original compiler likely emitted identical inlined blocks.
For descriptor tables terminated by a sentinel entry, try pointer iteration over the descriptor struct before assuming the source used an index counter.
When editing a function that shares helpers or layout assumptions with a fully matched neighbor, run checkdiff on both functions.
naming_conventions: New local types use the existing lbHeap_ prefix plus role names: lbHeap_HeapDesc and lbHeap_HeapOffsetView.
New helpers use lbHeap_ prefix and action-oriented names: lbHeap_ResetHeap, lbHeap_DestroyOffsetViewIfCreated, and lbHeap_CreateOffsetViewIfDestroyed.
Predicate helper names encode the heap status condition being handled with IfCreated and IfDestroyed.
Unknown globals kept address-based names, such as lbHeap_803BA380 and lbHeap_80431FA0, while newly introduced types/helpers documented their inferred role.
Loop and pointer locals were renamed by phase or role, including destroy_i, bounds_i, create_i, destroy_view, create_view, bounds_heap, main_heap, and aram_heap.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use a padded overlay struct to reach a struct Heap at a fixed offset from lbHeap_80431FA0: struct lbHeap_HeapOffsetView { u8 pad[0x10]; struct Heap heap; }.
Use a byte-offset macro, lbHeap_GetHeapOffsetView(offset), with heap_offset initialized to 0x38 and incremented by sizeof(struct Heap) in create/destroy loops.
Use static inline helpers for reset/create/destroy blocks so the C stays structured while preserving inlined codegen opportunities.
Unroll six lbHeap_ResetHeap calls in lbHeap_80015F3C instead of using the previous for loop.
Process heap bounds in pairs by assigning bounds_heap = &heap_array[bounds_i * 2 - 2], handling it, then incrementing bounds_heap once to handle the adjacent heap.
Walk lbHeap_803BA380 with a struct lbHeap_HeapDesc* desc and a sentinel condition while ((curr_idx = desc->idx) != 6).
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code at 67.00%, +0.01% and +544 bytes for this PR.
Bot report: main/melee/lb/lbheap lbHeap_80015F3C became a new full match, +21 bytes, 96.29% -> 100.00%.
Bot report: main/melee/lb/lbheap lbHeap_80015900 improved by +107 bytes, 81.08% -> 96.48%.
The PR was closed without merge in the provided GitHub metadata, and the slice does not explain why.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that touched src/melee/lb/lbheap.c after closed-unmerged PR #2497 to see whether this lbheap matching work was superseded or merged differently.
Search current master for lbHeap_HeapOffsetView, lbHeap_GetHeapOffsetView, and lbHeap_ResetHeap to see whether the offset-view/helper tactic survived.
Inspect subsequent history of lbHeap_80015900 for final matching strategy and whether the heaps 2-5 bounds scan remained.
Look for PRs mentioning lbHeap_80015F3C 100% match or lbHeap_803BA380 descriptor iteration.
Compare later checkdiff reports for main/melee/lb/lbheap to validate whether the bot-reported gains persisted.
